concerns.text: T012 (validation.py) and T013 (ingestion.py) are listed as implementation tasks, but T010 and T011 are listed as 'Tests' to be written FIRST. The task order implies T012/T013 must be implemented before T010/T011 can be written (to have code to test), violating the 'Tests FIRST' TDD requirement stated in the section header. T010/T011 must precede T012/T013 in the execution list.

T015 (flag users as gamified) is listed after T013 (ingestion) and T014 (aggregation). However, T013 explicitly states it must 'Validate gamified_app_usage tags' and T014 aggregates based on 'weekly_adherence_flag'. If T015 (the logic to derive the flag) is not implemented before T013 and T014, those tasks cannot function. T015 must precede T013 and T014.

T021 (VIF calculation) is listed after T020 (Fit mixed-effects model). VIF is a prerequisite check to determine if the model can be fitted with the current variables (FR-002). If VIF > 5, the model specification changes. T021 must precede T020 to ensure the correct model formula is used.

T024 (Count dropout events) is listed after T025 (Kaplan-Meier/Cox). FR-009 and the Edge Case section state the system MUST halt survival analysis if events < 10. T025 cannot run safely without the check in T024. T024 must precede T025.

T030 (Generate report) is listed before T031 (Sensitivity analysis). The report (T030) must contain the sensitivity analysis results (FR-005, SC-005). T031 must execute and produce the data before T030 can include it in the final artifact.

T016 (Error handling for Data Insufficiency) is listed after T017 (Generate merged CSV). The error handling for insufficient data (FR-008, Edge Cases) must occur during ingestion/aggregation (T013/T014) to prevent the generation of an invalid CSV (T017). T016 logic must be integrated before T017 runs.
